description: Mathematical functions. Provides access to the C standard math library functions for floating-point math operations.

functions.name: ceil

returns.description: The smallest integer value greater than or equal to x, as a float

functions.description: Return the ceiling of x as a float, the smallest integer value greater than or equal to x.

functions.name: floor

returns.description: The largest integer value less than or equal to x, as a float

functions.description: Return the floor of x as a float, the largest integer value less than or equal to x.

functions.name: fabs

returns.description: The absolute value of x

functions.description: Return the absolute value of x.

functions.name: factorial

returns.description: x factorial (x!)

functions.description: Return x factorial. Raises ValueError if x is not integral or is negative.

functions.name: fmod

returns.description: The remainder of x / y as defined by the C library fmod

functions.description: Return fmod(x, y), as defined by the platform C library. The intent is that the result has the same sign as x. Note: the Python expression x % y may not return the same result.

functions.name: frexp

returns.description: A pair (m, e) where x == m * 2**e, m is a float and e is an integer

functions.description: Return the mantissa and exponent of x as the pair (m, e). m is a float and e is an integer such that x == m * 2**e exactly.

functions.name: fsum

params.name: iterable
params.description: An iterable of float values to sum

returns.description: An accurate floating-point sum of values in the iterable

functions.description: Return an accurate floating-point sum of values in the iterable. Avoids loss of precision by tracking multiple intermediate partial sums.

functions.name: isinf

returns.description: True if x is positive or negative infinity, False otherwise

functions.description: Check if the float x is positive or negative infinity.

functions.name: isnan

returns.description: True if x is NaN (not a number), False otherwise

functions.description: Check if the float x is NaN (not a number).

functions.name: ldexp
functions.description: Return x * (2**i). This is essentially the inverse of frexp().

functions.name: modf

returns.description: A pair (fractional_part, integer_part), both with the same sign as x and are floats

functions.description: Return the fractional and integer parts of x. Both results carry the sign of x and are floats.

functions.name: trunc

returns.description: x truncated to an integer

functions.description: Return the Real value x truncated to an Integral (usually a long integer). Uses x.__trunc__().

functions.name: exp

returns.description: e raised to the power x (e**x)

functions.description: Return e raised to the power x, where e = 2.718281... is the base of natural logarithms.

functions.name: log

params.name: base
params.description: Logarithm base. If not supplied, returns the natural logarithm (base e).

returns.description: The logarithm of x to the given base, or the natural logarithm if base is not given

functions.description: With one argument, return the natural logarithm of x (to base e). With two arguments, return the logarithm of x to the given base, calculated as log(x)/log(base).

functions.name: log10

returns.description: The base-10 logarithm of x

functions.description: Return the base-10 logarithm of x. This is usually more accurate than log(x, 10).

functions.name: log1p

returns.description: The natural logarithm of 1+x (base e)

functions.description: Return the natural logarithm of 1+x (base e). The result is computed in a way which is accurate for x near zero.

functions.name: pow

returns.description: x raised to the power y

functions.description: Return x raised to the power y. Exceptional cases follow IEEE 754 standard as far as possible.

functions.name: sqrt

returns.description: The square root of x

functions.description: Return the square root of x.

functions.name: acos

returns.description: The arc cosine of x, in radians

functions.description: Return the arc cosine of x, in radians.

functions.name: asin

returns.description: The arc sine of x, in radians

functions.description: Return the arc sine of x, in radians.

functions.name: atan

returns.description: The arc tangent of x, in radians

functions.description: Return the arc tangent of x, in radians.

functions.name: atan2

returns.description: The arc tangent of y/x, in radians, with correct quadrant

functions.description: Return atan(y / x), in radians. The result is between -pi and pi. The vector in the plane from the origin to point (x, y) makes this angle with the positive X axis. Unlike atan(y/x), the signs of both inputs are known to atan2, so it can compute the correct quadrant.

functions.name: cos

returns.description: The cosine of x

functions.description: Return the cosine of x radians.

functions.name: sin

returns.description: The sine of x

functions.description: Return the sine of x radians.

functions.name: tan

returns.description: The tangent of x

functions.description: Return the tangent of x radians.

functions.name: degrees

returns.description: The angle in degrees

functions.description: Convert angle x from radians to degrees.

functions.name: radians

returns.description: The angle in radians

functions.description: Convert angle x from degrees to radians.

functions.name: hypot

params.name: x
params.description: One side of the right triangle

params.name: y
params.description: Other side of the right triangle

returns.description: The Euclidean norm, sqrt(x*x + y*y)

functions.description: Return the Euclidean norm, sqrt(x*x + y*y). This is the length of the vector from the origin to point (x, y).

functions.name: copysign

returns.description: x with the sign of y

functions.description: Return x with the sign of y. On a platform that supports signed zeros, copysign(1.0, -0.0) returns -1.0.
